title: BetaXiv Document (AIDoc)
description: Versioned contract between the betaxiv-documenter skill (writer) and the BetaXiv VS Code extension (reader). An AIDoc is a lightweight, agent-authored artifact about a paper (a comparison table, a method flowchart, a derivation, …) shown alongside the summary. Both sides validate against this file. Unlike the summary, blocks may include declarative `table` and `diagram` (Mermaid) blocks — the agent declares, the extension renders. The extension never generates content or launches an agent.

listItem.description: A list item: a plain string, or an object with nested sub-items (for tab-like indentation / outlines). Sub-lists may be ordered or unordered independently.

block.description: One ordered content block. paragraph/bullets/formula/figure behave exactly as in a summary; table and diagram are doc-only declarative blocks.

text.description: A section title. Use this for section/subsection headings — do NOT fake headings with a bold paragraph.

level.description: 2 = section (default), 3 = subsection. The document title comes from doc.title (level 1); headings start at 2.

text.description: Flowing prose. Inline emphasis supported: **bold**, `code`, and inline math $…$ (KaTeX).

ordered.description: true → numbered (ordered) list; omitted/false → bulleted list.

text.description: A display equation written in LaTeX, rendered as real math via KaTeX (no $ delimiters; the body only). Use \frac, ^, _, \sqrt, \sum, Greek (\alpha), etc.

label.description: Must match a figures[].label; the extension crops that figure from the source PDF and renders it inline here.

caption.description: Optional table caption shown above the table.

header.description: Column headers. Cells support the same inline emphasis as paragraph text (**bold**, `code`, $math$).

rows.description: Row-major cells. Each row is an array of string cells (inline markup allowed). Ragged rows are tolerated by the renderer.

mermaid.description: Mermaid diagram source, rendered to SVG client-side. Use ONLY built-in diagram types so nothing is lazily fetched under CSP: flowchart/graph, sequenceDiagram, classDiagram, stateDiagram, erDiagram, pie, xychart-beta. Use xychart-beta for BAR and simple line charts; for SCATTER plots, log axes, or error bars use a `chart` block instead. e.g. 'flowchart LR\n  A[Input] --> B[Encoder] --> C[Decoder] --> D[Output]'.

caption.description: Optional caption shown under the diagram.

oneOf.description: A scientific plot (scatter or numeric-x line) drawn by the extension as a crisp vector SVG from declarative data — supports log axes, error bars, multiple series, and a legend (what Mermaid's xychart cannot do). Use a `diagram` (xychart-beta) for categorical BAR charts instead.

kind.description: scatter: markers only. line: markers connected by a line, sorted by x.

alt.description: Plain-language description of the chart for accessibility.

legend.description: Show a series legend. Defaults to true when there is more than one series.

scale.description: log requires strictly-positive values on that axis.

domain.description: Axis range. Omit or use "auto" to derive it from the data (incl. error bars).

chartErrorBar.description: Symmetric +/- error (a number) or an absolute interval {low, high} in data units. Use {low, high} on log axes so the interval never crosses zero.

marker.description: Marker shape; defaults are auto-assigned per series so shape (not just color) distinguishes them.

color.description: Optional series color as a hex code (#rgb or #rrggbb). Omit to use the auto-assigned palette color. Restricted to hex for safety.

bbox.description: Normalized [x0, y0, x1, y1] in 0..1, origin top-left, measured against the upright cropBox page (PDF.js `view`, rotation applied) — the same frame the extension renders and crops. Ground it on that page image (see the skill's crop_helper.py). Null if unknown (the extension then shows caption + page only).

schemaVersion.description: Schema contract version. Pinned so reader and writer never silently diverge.

id.description: Stable slug, unique within this paper's docs directory. Becomes the filename: .betaxiv/docs/<contentId>/<id>.doc.json.

title.description: Human title shown in the AIDocs dropdown and as the document heading.

kind.description: Free-form category hint, e.g. note | report | comparison | derivation. Purely informational.

sourcePath.description: Path to the source PDF this doc is about, relative to the workspace root (e.g. papers/attention.pdf).

description.description: Optional one-line summary shown under the title in the dropdown. Required key, nullable value.

blocks.description: Ordered content blocks rendered top-to-bottom. Figures appear inline via a figure block referencing a figures[] entry by label.

figures.description: Optional catalog of figures/tables cropped from the SOURCE PDF (page + bbox). Same mechanism as summaries — the extension crops the real image; the agent never draws. External data must go in table/diagram blocks instead. May be omitted or [].

agent.description: The agent harness that produced this doc, e.g. Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI.

model.description: Model identifier if known, else null.
